claim_boundary: Constrained 1D gamma_sub recovery only; synthetic benchmark, calibrated/tight priors, no arbitrary full-field or experimental-validation claim.

claims.claim: Sparse terminal observations are insufficient for unique recovery of arbitrary complete hidden fields in the frozen 1D benchmark.
claims.forbidden_wording: Sparse-port observations uniquely recover all hidden fields, or the audit proves universal non-identifiability for every device model.
claims.limitation: Correlation and failed recovery in this configured synthetic benchmark do not prove a universal mathematical impossibility theorem.
claims.manuscript_sentence: In the frozen synthetic benchmark, sparse terminal signals constrain integrated conductance but do not uniquely recover the complete thermal, defect, phase, and conductivity fields, motivating inverse target reduction.

claims.claim: gamma_sub is recoverable as a reduced inverse target when microphysics is fixed and confounders are fixed or tightly bounded.
claims.forbidden_wording: gamma_sub is globally or unconditionally identifiable, or the result is a measured device parameter.
claims.limitation: The target is a lumped effective thermal-dissipation parameter in a 1D synthetic model, not an unconditional material constant.
claims.manuscript_sentence: With microphysical parameters fixed and nuisance priors tightly bounded, the reduced inverse recovers the effective substrate-dissipation parameter gamma_sub, including off-grid targets after continuous local refinement.

claims.claim: T_sw is the dominant audited confounder and must be calibrated or tightly bounded before gamma_sub inversion.
claims.forbidden_wording: A real device requires or achieves 0.1 K calibration accuracy.
claims.limitation: The approximately 0.1 K marker is benchmark-specific under the configured 15 percent median-error criterion, not a real-device calibration specification.
claims.manuscript_sentence: The response-surface audit and 270-case ODE spot-check support a benchmark-specific residual T_sw tolerance near 0.1 K under the configured error criterion; larger mismatch sharply degrades gamma_sub recovery.

claims.claim: Noise, seed, observation-count, and off-grid audits support gamma_sub recovery only inside the calibrated or tightly bounded prior region.
claims.forbidden_wording: The inverse is robust without calibration, or aggregate averages establish unconditional noise robustness.
claims.limitation: Wide T_sw mismatch fails systematically; aggregate robustness must not hide the failed prior region.
claims.manuscript_sentence: Across the audited noise, seed, observation-count, and off-grid cases, recovery remains stable only after prior control, whereas wide T_sw mismatch defines a reproducible failure region.

claims.claim: Protocol selection is a secondary robustness enhancer after T_sw calibration, not a substitute for calibration.
claims.forbidden_wording: The protocol is experimentally validated, globally optimal, or sufficient without calibrated priors.
claims.limitation: The main protocol result contains 720 synthetic ODE-backed cases under calibrated priors; a broader 2400-case stress audit is supplementary and not main-figure ready.
claims.manuscript_sentence: After T_sw calibration, the 720-case ODE-backed sequential validation identifies calibrated_multi_pulse_to_ltp_ltd as the best tested protocol, with success rate 1.0 and maximum relative error 0.1111; protocol gain remains secondary to calibration gain.

claims.allowed_sentence: No provenance-backed digitized external curve is currently available; no experimental-validation claim is made.
claims.claim: Provenance-backed external quantitative validation has not been completed.
claims.forbidden_wording: Experimental validation or fitted public curves have been completed.

claims.allowed_sentence: The strict CV/interface audit fails and exposes scaling, coordinate, and optimization repair targets.
claims.claim: The v10 CV multidomain neural solver passes its strict field/interface gate.

claims.allowed_sentence: Local block recovery is mixed, and rank-deficient protocol selection invalidates a solved multi-parameter inverse claim.
claims.claim: The v10 active terminal inverse solves all material-specific parameter blocks.
claims.forbidden_wording: Full nonlinear multi-parameter inverse is solved.

claims.allowed_sentence: Segmented terminal currents increase the local Jacobian rank for a three-parameter conductivity-profile basis.
claims.claim: Segmented terminals improve low-dimensional local observability in the synthetic y-z forward model.
claims.forbidden_wording: Segmented terminals recover arbitrary 2D hidden fields.

claims.allowed_sentence: The algorithm comparison remains blocked by the failed P1 gate.
claims.claim: Full STL or universal Fourier/F-SPS superiority is supported on v10.
claims.forbidden_wording: Full STL reproduction or universal Fourier/F-SPS superiority.

figures.caption: Terminal correlations are high for integrated conductivity-related quantities, but port-only recovery of the complete hidden fields fails in the frozen synthetic benchmark.
figures.title: Sparse-port identifiability boundary

figures.caption: Panel a shows the synthetic gamma_sub-T_sw objective ridge; panel b directly compares true and continuously estimated off-grid gamma_sub in 36 calibrated synthetic cases. Calibration or tight prior control is required before reduced inversion.
figures.title: Constrained gamma_sub recovery and T_sw confounding

figures.caption: Recoverable and failed regions are shown for the configured synthetic prior, noise, and observation protocols.
figures.title: Recoverability phase diagram under prior width, noise, and observation count

figures.caption: Selected response-surface predictions are compared against fresh simulator-backed anchors; the dense surface is not represented as independent ODE solves.
figures.title: Response-surface anchor verification

figures.caption: In 720 synthetic ODE-backed cases under calibrated priors, the calibrated sequential protocol clears the configured error gate; it is not an experimentally validated protocol.
figures.title: Calibrated sequential protocol validation

figures.caption: Seeded noise audits retain both the calibrated-prior success region and the wide-T_sw-mismatch failure region.
figures.title: Conditional statistical robustness and failed wide-prior region
